title: Axor Lab Publication
description: The immutable public record of a run. Provenance is MULTIDIMENSIONAL: execution origin, integrity, and reproduction are independent axes, not one mutually-exclusive status. A Lab-executed run can also be independently reproduced; a self-reported run can later accrue reproductions. The card composes them: 'Lab-executed · signed bundle · independently live-reproduced ×4'.

bundle_ref.description: content hash of the bundle this publishes

immutable.description: publications never change in place; a revision is a new publication linking back

origin.description: AXIS 1 — where it executed. local/external are honest about not running on Lab infra.

integrity.description: AXIS 2 — artifact integrity. hash_verified = content hashes check out; signed = detached signature by the author key.

claims.description: claims are typed by what actually supports them — the exact/statistical boundary is structural, not prose (see claims.md)

kind.description: exactly_replayable = a verdict over a fixed trace ('on trace T, axor-core 0.4.2 returns DENY because recipient is untrusted-derived'). statistically_reproducible = an aggregate over live runs ('in 30 live repetitions, cooperation 0.88 [CI]'). A behavioral delta is NEVER exactly_replayable.

support_ref.description: trace ref for exact claims; aggregate ref for statistical claims

limitations.description: stated, not hidden — including the interested-result caveat (this measures Axor's own enforcement) and the replay-counterfactual limit

citation.description: BibTeX including kernel version + config hash

reproductions_ref.description: pointer to an append-only attestation log (attestation/v1 entries). The publication is immutable; reproductions accrue in a separate append-only stream, each an independent signed attestation referencing this publication_id. Resolves the immutable-body vs growing-count tension.
